---
title: "Git-HTTP kennwortlos"
author: "Wolfgang Höfer"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: scrreprt
    lang: "de"
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage[top=2cm, bottom=2.5cm]{geometry}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \usepackage{changepage}
        \setlist[description]{style=nextline, leftmargin=2.5em}
        \newcommand{\cmd}[1]{\texttt{#1}\xspace}
jupyter: python3
---


# Anleitung[^cc]

[^cc]: Dieses Werk steht unter der **Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC BY-SA 4.0)**. 

Eine unsichere, aber schnelle Variante ist ein 
Git-Server, der http ohne Kennwort unterstützt.

Nachfolgend die relevanten Dateien:

* docker-compose.yml
* nginx.conf
* Dockerfile
* startup.sh

Der Start und Einsatz des Containers wird danach beschrieben


**docker-compose.yml**  
```bash
version: '3.8'

services:
  git-nginx:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: always
    container_name: git-nginx-container
    ports:
      - "8080:80"     # HTTP Port
    volumes:
      - ./repo:/git
      - ./repositories:/var/www/git/repositories # nicht klar wofür
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf  
```


**nginx.conf**  
```bash
worker_processes  1;

error_log  /var/log/nginx/error.log;
pid /run/nginx.pid;
user root;

events {
    worker_connections  1024;
}

http {
    server {
        listen  *:80;

        root /www/empty/;
        index index.html;

        server_name $hostname;
        access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log;

        #error_page 404 /404.html;

        #auth_basic            "Restricted";
        #auth_basic_user_file  /www/htpasswd;

        location ~ /git(/.*) {
            # Set chunks to unlimited, as the bodies can be huge
            client_max_body_size            0;

            fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME /usr/libexec/git-core/git-http-backend;
            include fastcgi_params;
            fastcgi_param GIT_HTTP_EXPORT_ALL "";
            fastcgi_param GIT_PROJECT_ROOT /git;
            fastcgi_param PATH_INFO $1;

            fastcgi_param REQUEST_METHOD $request_method;
            fastcgi_param QUERY_STRING $query_string;

            # Forward REMOTE_USER as we want to know when we are authenticated
            fastcgi_param   REMOTE_USER     $remote_user;
            fastcgi_pass    unix:/run/fcgi.sock;
        }
    }
```

**Dockerfile**  
```bash
FROM alpine:latest

MAINTAINER Anthony Hogg anthony@hogg.fr

# The container listens on port 80, map as needed
EXPOSE 80

# Der Ort im Image, wo die Repos liegen werden
# Da wir ein benanntes Volume wollen, überschreiben wir 
# das im compose-File!
# VOLUME ["/git"]

# We need the following:
# - git-daemon, because that gets us the git-http-backend CGI script
# - fcgiwrap, because that is how nginx does CGI
# - spawn-fcgi, to launch fcgiwrap and to create the unix socket
# - nginx, because it is our frontend
RUN apk add --update nginx && \
    apk add --update git-daemon && \
    apk add --update fcgiwrap && \
    apk add --update spawn-fcgi && \
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/*

COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# launch fcgiwrap via spawn-fcgi; launch nginx in the foreground
# so the container doesn't die on us; supposedly we should be
# using supervisord or something like that instead, but this
# will do
CMD spawn-fcgi -s /run/fcgi.sock /usr/bin/fcgiwrap && \
    nginx -g "daemon off;"
```

**startup.sh**  
```bash
#!/bin/sh

chmod -R 777 /git
cd /git
git init --bare ${1}.git
cd ${1}.git
git branch -m main
git config http.receivepack true 

exit 0
```


Der Container wirs über folgenden Befehl erstellt und gestartet:

```bash
docker-compose up -d
```

Das Skript *startup.sh* erstellt im Container ein neues Repository. 
Es kann entweder vom Hostsystem oder aus dem Container gestartet werden:

**Hostsystem**  
```bash
docker exec -it git-nginx-container /startup.sh projekt  # Reponame OHNE .git angeben
```

**Im Container**  
```bash
docker exec -it git-nginx-container /bin/sh
./startup.sh projekt  # OHNE Endung .git
```

*Verlassen des Containers*

```bash
strg + pq
```

**Clonen**  
Zum Clonen wird dann verwendet

```bash
git clone http://<IP>:8080/git/projekt.git
```
